articles.source: Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism
articles.adaptedFrom: Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism
articles.title: Should Children Under 16 Be Banned from Social Media? UK Government Asks the Public
articles.summary: The UK government launched a public consultation in March 2026 to explore whether children under 16 should be prohibited from using social media platforms. This move comes as over three-quarters of British adults expressed concern about fake news and misinformation spreading online. Citizens and experts are being invited to share opinions before any legislation is passed. The debate is significant because millions of young people rely on social media daily for communication, entertainment, and learning — yet health professionals warn it may also pose risks to their mental wellbeing and personal safety online.

lv1: What age group could be banned from social media under the UK's new proposal? (英國新建議可能禁止哪個年齡組別使用社交媒體？)
What are two things young people use social media for every day? (年輕人每天使用社交媒體做哪兩件事？)
lv2: Why do you think the UK government decided to ask the public for opinions before writing a new law? (你認為英國政府為何在制定新法律前先徵詢公眾意見？)
What are some ways that social media can be both useful and dangerous for students your age? (社交媒體對你這個年齡的學生來說，有哪些既有用又危險的地方？)
lv3: Should governments have the authority to restrict which online platforms young people can access? Consider arguments both for and against such a policy. (政府是否應有權限制年輕人能使用哪些網絡平台？請考慮支持和反對這項政策的論點。)

If Hong Kong introduced a similar ban on social media for under-16s, what would be the impact on students' social lives, mental health, and access to information? (如果香港為16歲以下人士引入類似的社交媒體禁令，對學生的社交生活、精神健康及獲取資訊會有什麼影響？)

articles.title: Argentina Court Rules That Goldfish Can Feel — and Gives Them Legal Rights
articles.summary: In a ground-breaking ruling, an Argentine court officially recognised two goldfish as sentient beings in July 2026 — creatures capable of experiencing feelings and sensations. This legal decision means the fish are now entitled to certain protections under the law. Historically, such recognition has only been extended to larger animals like apes or elephants. Animal rights advocates welcomed the verdict as a major step forward. Scientists have long debated the emotional capacity of fish, making this case a fascinating intersection of law, science, and ethics that could influence how courts worldwide treat animals in future.

lv1: What kind of animal did the court in Argentina give legal rights to? (阿根廷法院給予了哪種動物法律權利？)
What does the word 'sentient' mean in this article? (在這篇文章中，「有感知能力」是什麼意思？)
lv2: Why might it matter legally whether an animal can feel pain or emotions? (從法律角度來看，動物能否感受痛苦或情緒為何很重要？)
How could this court decision affect how people keep fish as pets or use them in scientific experiments? (這項法庭裁決可能如何影響人們養魚作寵物或將魚用於科學實驗的方式？)
lv3: Should all living creatures, regardless of size or species, receive legal protections from harm? What are the practical challenges of implementing such rights globally? (無論大小或物種，所有生物是否都應受到法律保護免受傷害？在全球實施這些權利有哪些實際挑戰？)

If Hong Kong adopted similar animal sentience laws, how might local industries such as the fishing trade, wet markets, and research laboratories be affected? (如果香港採納類似的動物感知能力法律，漁業、街市和研究實驗室等本地行業會受到怎樣的影響？)

articles.title: 2026年DSE放榜　24名狀元誕生　自修生人數破新高
articles.summary: 2026年香港中學文憑試（DSE）於7月15日放榜，共有24名考生在至少6科取得5**兼公民科達標，成為本屆狀元。今年報考人數上升，自修生達12,143人，按年增逾1,900人，創近年新高。惟考評局發現7名考生涉嫌作弊，全部科目成績遭取消。教育局局長蔡若蓮親赴學校為同學打氣，強調考試成績不能衡量一個人的全部價值，鼓勵考生積極展望未來。

lv1: 教育局局長探訪學校是為了做什麼？
lv2: 你認為自修生人數不斷增加，反映了香港社會哪些變化或需求？
考試作弊的後果十分嚴重，為什麼同學仍會冒險這樣做？
lv3: 「成績單不能定義人生價值」——你認為香港社會是否過分強調考試成績？這種風氣對學生有何影響？
如果你有機會改革DSE制度，你會作出哪些改變，使它更全面地反映學生的能力與潛力？

articles.title: 泰國曼谷旅遊區酒吧深夜大火　28人死亡逾70人受傷
articles.summary: 泰國首都曼谷一個著名旅遊區的酒吧，深夜突然發生嚴重火災，造成至少28人死亡、71人受傷。事發時現場聚集大批遊客及本地居民，火勢蔓延迅速，逃生期間秩序大亂。消防員趕抵救援，但傷亡仍然慘重。當局已展開調查，查明起火原因。事件令社會各界再次關注公眾娛樂場所的消防設施是否完善，以及緊急應變措施是否足夠。

lv1: 今次曼谷大火造成多少人死亡？
消防員趕到現場後主要做了什麼？
lv2: 為什麼在人多的夜間娛樂場所發生火災會特別容易造成大量傷亡？
從今次事件中，我們可以學到哪些保障公共安全的重要教訓？
lv3: 政府應如何加強監管公眾娛樂場所的消防安全標準，以減少類似悲劇發生的風險？
旅遊業是泰國的主要經濟來源，這類意外對當地旅遊業及遊客的信心會造成什麼長遠影響？
